page: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g is a virtual server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is configured with an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor featuring 2 shared vCPUs, which map to 1 physical core and 2 threads. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Storage is limited to 1 GB of network-attached local storage, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks show a stark contrast between single-threaded and multi-threaded capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier results, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Conversely, due to its shared CPU allocation and single physical core, multi-core metrics are weak, with stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and PassMark database operations all falling into the bottom 25%.

Memory performance is moderate, with large-block read and write bandwidth registering in the average tier, while small-block cached reads are weak. Static web serving throughput and small LLM text generation also perform at average levels. Qualitatively, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy workloads that do not require sustained, multi-threaded CPU performance. It is best suited for caching, lightweight web serving, and background processing tasks.
description: The Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. While its single-threaded performance is highly competitive, ranking in the top 10% for single-core stress-ng and compression benchmarks, its multi-core capabilities and database operations fall into the bottom 25%. With 1 GB of network storage and average memory bandwidth for large blocks, this configuration is best suited for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads such as caching, lightweight web serving, and basic background tasks where high memory capacity is required without intensive multi-threaded processing.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 1 GB of network storage, delivering strong single-core but limited multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-2c-16g is a memory-optimized server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining high memory capacity with strong single-threaded performance for lightweight, memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
1 GB of network-attached local storage and one public IPv4 address
Average performance for large-block memory bandwidth and static web serving
Best suited for memory-intensive, low-compute workloads like caching
